title: Doré / Goya / Nordic Gloomy / Munch Troll / Burzum Hvis
mood: stark romanticism, forest horror, folkloric dread, sacred darkness

influences.name: Gustave Doré
keys: dense cross-hatching etched into shadow
cathedral-scale drama in natural scenes
silver-grey light cutting through black masses
figures dwarfed by sublime verticality
intricate foliage rendered as black lace

influences.name: Francisco Goya
keys: earth-brown and black pigment, barely any hue
figures emerging from mud-dark backgrounds
Saturn-eye vacancy, open mouths mid-scream
war-pastoral — violence in ordinary fields
paint applied like it was smeared in grief

influences.name: Nordic Romantic painters (Dahl, Tidemand, Fearnley)
influences.role: landscape mood, nature as spiritual threat
keys: midnight blue above frozen farm fields
birch groves at the edge of cleared land
snowlight — the only illumination, cold and flat
farmsteads as small warm rectangles against black forest
sky that feels heavier than the earth
waterfalls as white gashes in dark rock

influences.name: Edvard Munch (troll phase)
keys: faces that have become landscape
the troll is the tree is the old woman
colour reduced to bile-green, bruise-purple, corpse-white
figures that look like they grew there, not arrived
anxiety rendered as weather
children watching something in the dark that adults cannot see

influences.name: Burzum — Hvis lyset tar oss (1994)
keys: image so dark detail requires searching
grain as a spiritual layer — not noise, but veil
forest canopy closing over a path that leads nowhere good
the cover asks: what if the light leaves and does not come back
monochrome with one cold hue bleeding through
beauty that is also a warning

lighting: light arrives as survival, not comfort
single cold source — moon, a window, distant fire
shadows eat more than half the canvas
no fill light — darkness is the default state
silver rim-light on trees and figures
light from below suggests ritual or wrongness
textures: heavy grain — film or printing-plate origin
wet bark surface on all organic forms
old paper stain beneath the image
frost on glass as compositional element
compositional_rules: vertical dominance — trees, cliffs, standing figures
human element is small and possibly lost
frame edges can be dark — vignette is structural not decorative
horizon very low OR hidden entirely by canopy
negative space is active — forest darkness is a character
symmetry that is almost right but subtly wrong
subject_affinities: snow-covered fields, single farmhouse far back
standing stones or cairns at midnight
figures in cloaks moving away from viewer
animals that might be something else
bare winter branches against overcast sky
frozen river or lake with fog above
bridges over gorges, no destination visible

prompt_fragments.style_suffix: in the style of Gustave Doré engravings and Goya black paintings, Nordic romantic landscape painting, Edvard Munch trollish horror, Burzum black metal artwork, heavy grain, cross-hatch texture, stark romanticism
prompt_fragments.lighting_suffix: lit by cold moonlight only, deep shadow consuming foreground, silver rim-light on organic forms, no fill light
prompt_fragments.mood_suffix: atmosphere of sacred dread, folkloric unease, the forest as spiritual threat, beauty that is also a warning
prompt_fragments.negative_prompt: colorful, bright, cheerful, sunny, saturated, clean, digital smooth, anime, cartoon, photorealistic skin, stock photo
